title: ranunculus nemorosus subsp. questieri
intro: Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. questieri, also known as the Questier's woodland crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of ranunculin and other alkaloids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a tincture or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes.
